_comment: Probe 2B fallback — command-type hooks that bypass $COHERENCE_PORT env propagation entirely. Each hook invokes the agent-coherence-hook-client console script which reads <workspace>/.coherence/server.pid for the port and hook.secret for the Bearer token, then makes the HTTP POST. Use only if Phase E.0 probe 2A demonstrates $CLAUDE_ENV_FILE propagation is unreliable on the target claude version. See docs/probes/2026-05-17-phase-e0-probe-procedure.md.

PreToolUse._comment: KTD-N H4 mitigation — the model routes around a denied/warned Read via `bash cat plan.md`. Without a Bash PreToolUse matcher, that routing pattern silently bypasses the stale-read warning surface, leaving the plugin's coherence guarantee intact only for the literal Read tool. The pre-bash client subcommand extracts tracked-artifact paths from the Bash command string and forwards them to /hooks/pre-bash on the coordinator.

KTD-N H4 mitigation — Grep is another bypass route: a user-initiated or model-initiated grep over a directory containing tracked artifacts (CLAUDE.md, plan.md, etc.) should surface stale-read warnings for artifacts the session hasn't freshened since peer commits. The pre-grep client subcommand extracts the search root and forwards to /hooks/pre-grep on the coordinator, which enumerates registry-known tracked artifacts under the prefix.
